---
title: "Audio Batch Workflows"
subtitle: "`extract_audio_files()` and `extract_audio_dir()` - clip matrices and collapsed summaries."
sidebar: concepts
---

## What it is

Audio batch workflows are ergonomic wrappers for short WAV clips. They read files, extract selected low-level features, resample each feature space to a shared resolution, and optionally collapse each file to summary statistics.

## Invariants

- `resolution_s` must be positive.
- Unknown feature names raise before extraction.
- Selected feature names are prefixed in output columns.
- `collapse` supports `mean`, `sd`, `min`, and `max`.
- `as_dataframe=True` requires pandas.

## What it refuses

- Unsupported collapse statistics.
- Unknown selected feature names.
- Non-WAV paths passed to `AudioStimulus.from_wav()`.

## What it doesn't do

The batch workflow is not a general media decoder. It expects WAV files and delegates feature computation to registered low-level audio helpers.

## Available batch feature names

```{python}
from natural_features.workflows.audio_batch import _FEATURES

sorted(_FEATURES)
```

## See also

[Extract audio features from WAV files](../cookbook/audio-batch-from-wav.qmd)
